title: Add wet crossover and drying-track detectors
summary: Pirelli explicitly describes crossover points between slicks, intermediates, and wets, with lap-time inflation thresholds and wide crossover windows on inters.
rationale: Pirelli explicitly describes crossover points between slicks, intermediates, and wets, with lap-time inflation thresholds and wide crossover windows on inters. Wet-race pit calls are partly driver-led and differ from dry degradation logic, so a separate regime detector is likely orthogonal to current dry-focused heuristics.
implementation_hint: For rows on slick/inter/wet compounds, build normalized pace-loss features within race-lap and within compound, then flag crossover pressure when lap time inflates beyond dry/inter baselines. Add transitions such as slick->inter pressure, inter->slick drying pressure, and wet-compound overstay risk; train either a wet-only sidecar or explicit regime features.
expected_effect: Should especially help on rain-affected laps where current tyre-life windows are weak and decision boundaries are sharper.
risk: Few wet laps may make the regime sparse; use simple thresholds or shrinkage rather than a large wet-only model.
